channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Back-to-back hurricanes reshape 2024 campaign’s final stretch
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A pair of unwelcome and destructive guests named Helene and Milton have stormed their way into this year's presidential election.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 12 rescued from Colorado gold mine tourist site where elevator mishap killed 1 person
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were rescued Thursday night after being trapped for about six hours at the bottom of a former Colorado gold mine when an elevator malfunctioned at the tourist site, authorities said. One person died in the accident.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Residents clean up and figure out what's next after Milton
item.description: Florida residents are continuing to repair the damage caused by Hurricane Milton and figure out what to do next.
item.dc:creator: Julio Cortez, Kate Payne And Haven Daley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and wife of Robert F. Kennedy, has died
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy, who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: South Koreans are joyful after Han Kang wins Nobel Prize for literature
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Koreans reacted with joy and astonishment on Thursday after learning that homegrown writer Han Kang won the Nobel Prize in literature, an unexpected moment that stoked national pride about the country’s growing cultural influence.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton after it slammed Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: As Hurricane Milton barreled into and across Florida, it spawned dozens of tornadoes, knocked out power to millions, damaged homes, flooded neighborhoods and led to hundreds of rescues. But the damage wasn't as bad as had been feared. Gov.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Anti-Israel agitators deface Harvard statute day after Oct. 7 anniversary: ‘Bring the war home’
item.description: Just after the one­-year anniversary of the Oct. 7 massacre in Israel, demonstrators vandalized Harvard's campus in "an act of solidarity with the Palestinian resistance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KRON4

item.title: Hurricane Milton disrupts Yom Kippur plans for Jews in Florida
item.description: WINTER PARK, Florida (AP) — Many Jews worldwide will mark Yom Kippur in fasting and prayer at their synagogues this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Condemned inmate Richard Moore wants someone other than South Carolina's governor to decide clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate scheduled to be executed in just over three weeks is asking a federal judge to take away the power of granting clemency from the governor who is a former state attorney general and place it with a parole board.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A federal judge rejects a call to reopen voter registration in Georgia after Hurricane Helene
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A federal judge said Thursday that she won't order the presidential battleground state of Georgia to reopen voter registration for November’s elections despite recent disruptions to registration caused by Hurricane Helene. U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tampa woman killed by falling branch after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Investigators said that it appears that reconstruction efforts were being done in the area when the branch fell on the victim. So far, the death appears to be an accident.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Florida deputies rescue boy floating on piece of fence in floodwaters
item.description: A Florida boy floating in the flooded water in Tampa was rescued by sheriff's deputies on a patrol boat, authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pennsylvania father, sons accused of poaching nearly 50 deer
item.description: An investigation that lasted almost two years found that the Nelsons had been illegally taking whitetail bucks for several years, according to state game wardens.
item.dc:creator: Rian Bossler, NewsNation

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: A rare comet is expected to be visible to the naked eye across the Northern Hemisphere for several weeks in October.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: House Democrats' campaign chief defends vulnerable members keeping distance from Harris
item.description: NEW YORK — Rep. Suzan DelBene (D-Wash.), the House Democrats’ campaign chief, is defending vulnerable lawmakers who have kept their distance from Vice President Harris while campaigning.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Morgan Wallen Foundation, Second Harvest donates 150,000+ pounds of food
item.description: In addition to the hands-on work, the team also operates a Virtual Food Drive that has collected 100,000 meals.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Hagood, NewsNation

item.title: Prosecutors reviewing 'new evidence' in Marilyn Manson sexual abuse probe
item.description: The sex crimes division of the Los Angeles County District Attorney’s Office is reviewing new leads and evidence in the investigation into shock-rocker Marilyn Manson, who has been accused by multiple women, including “Westworld” actor Evan Rachel Wood, of sexual abuse, officials announced Wednesday
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: Police rescue 15 people from Tampa home after Milton knocks tree on house
item.description: Officers with the Tampa Police Department were sheltering a few miles when they were alerted to the incident via a 911 call.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, Fox 5

item.title: Immigrants brought to the US as children ask judges to keep protections against deportation
item.description: Immigrants who were brought to the U.S. illegally as children were among more than 200 demonstrators who gathered outside a federal courthouse in New Orleans where appellate judges heard arguments over a policy shielding them from deportation.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Mcgill And Jack Brook, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Taylor Swift donates $5 million to Hurricane Helene and Milton relief efforts
item.description: “This contribution will help communities rebuild and recover, providing essential food, clean water, and supplies to people affected by these devastating storms," Feeding America CEO Claire Babineaux-Fontenot said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: NC National Guard helicopter crew grounded after 'rotor wash' incident blows away Helene aid
item.description: Video of the incident appears to show the wind from the helicopter blowing over tents and scattering items in the air.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Polluted waste from Florida's fertilizer industry is in the path of Milton's fury
item.description: As Hurricane Milton pummeled Florida’s west coast with powerful winds and flooding rain, environmentalists worry it could scatter the polluted leftovers of the state’s phosphate fertilizer mining industry and other hazardous waste across the peninsula and into vulnerable waterways.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BIESECKER and JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Brown rejects calls to divest from companies in connection with pro-Palestinian protests on campus
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Brown University has rejected a proposal to divest from 10 companies that protesters say were facilitating the Israeli occupation of Palestinian territory.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Trump-Putin ties are back in the spotlight after new book describes calls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A new book's assertion that former President Donald Trump may have had as many as seven private phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving the White House has refocused attention on their politically fraught relationship and on Trump's sustained dialogue with w
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alaska’s Fat Bear Contest winner finishes ahead of the bear that killed her cub
item.description: After fans cast their votes online for their favorite chunky bear in tournament-style brackets, Grazer won her second Fat Bear Contest on Tuesday, defeating the behemoth that killed her cub.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, KRON4

item.title: Head of international shipping regulator says industry must do more to cut carbon pollution
item.description: HAMBURG, Germany (AP) — For years, the international shipping industry has been criticized for making little progress in reducing the carbon-belching pollution released from the fuels that vessels use in moving most of the cargo that people use every day, such as food, cars and clothing.
item.dc:creator: PETER PRENGAMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NASA video shows Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: Video taken from the International Space Station puts the massive size of Hurricane Milton into perspective.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: 2025 Met Gala theme revealed; LeBron James among co-chairs
item.description: The Metropolitan Museum Art has announced the theme for the 2025 Met Gala, formally called the Costume Institute Benefit. The annual spring event's theme will be “Superfine: Tailoring Black Style," according to Vogue. It was inspired by Monica L.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Landfall timeline: When will Hurricane Milton hit Florida?
item.description: Hurricane Milton continues to push closer to the Florida coastline, and the impacts begin well before landfall.
item.dc:creator: Leigh Spann, Fox 5

item.title: Will we see northern lights, power grid impacts amid 'severe' geomagnetic storm?
item.description: Earth is being hit by a strong solar radiation storm event that will be followed by a geomagnetic storm, space weather officials say.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, KRON4

item.title: Dolly Parton, Walmart announce millions in donations to Helene victims
item.description: East Tennessee's own Dolly Parton is expected to offer her assistance to help with the flood recovery efforts in Newport.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, WFLA, NBC
